purpose: Single source of truth for recurring project questions on this Odoo 17 workspace. Agents MUST consult this registry before answering 'how do we do X in this project'. Adding/changing entries requires explicit user approval - this protects determinism.

decisions.question: What is the project stack?
decisions.answer: Odoo 17 on Python {{STACK_LANGUAGE_VERSION}} (project venv at {{PYTHON_BIN}}), PostgreSQL, OWL frontend. Workspace root: {{WORKSPACE_ROOT}}. Database: {{DEFAULT_DB}}.

decisions.question: Which Python interpreter do we run for this project?
decisions.answer: Always use the project venv: {{PYTHON_BIN}} (Python {{STACK_LANGUAGE_VERSION}}). Never use a system-PATH `python`; it can resolve to an interpreter without the project's dependencies and silently diverge from the runtime. The venv path is exported in .codex/mcp.local.env so MCP wrappers, Odoo shell, and test runners stay aligned.

decisions.question: Where do custom and vendored addons live?
decisions.answer: Addon roots: {{ADDON_ROOTS_CSV}}. Run `codebase.workspace_status` for the live list.

decisions.question: Which Odoo 17 API decorators do we use?
decisions.answer: Methods are recordset by default in Odoo 17 — `@api.multi` is removed and must NOT be reintroduced. Use `@api.model` only for class-level methods that don't need a recordset. Use `@api.model_create_multi` to override `create()` so batch creates work. Call `ensure_one()` when a method assumes a single record.

decisions.question: What ORM calls are forbidden inside loops?
decisions.answer: Never call search(), browse(id), write(), or unlink() inside loops. Push filtering into domains, batch with mapped/filtered/read_group, and use search_count() instead of len(search()).

decisions.question: When may we use sudo()?
decisions.answer: Avoid sudo() unless clearly required. Document every sudo() call with a one-line reason. Default to standard ACL/record-rule security.

decisions.question: How do we verify Odoo 17 changes?
decisions.answer: Prefer the narrowest module-level check: odoo-bin -u <module> --stop-after-init -d {{DEFAULT_DB}}. For real-data smoke checks, use the realdata_test MCP run_smoke_test tool. For algorithm correctness, use eval_orm_expression + consistency_check_eval (the same expression must produce identical fingerprints across runs).

decisions.question: Which MCP do we use for which question?
decisions.answer: Codebase discovery, manifests, models, XML IDs => codebase. Read-only DB schema/data => postgres. Real-data smoke and algorithm verification => realdata_test. Never broad-read files before asking the codebase MCP.

decisions.question: Which language do we reply in?
decisions.answer: Reply in {{RESPONSE_LANGUAGE}} unless the user asks otherwise. Code comments stay in English.

decisions.question: Should rules and skills hard-code specific module names?
decisions.answer: No. Rules and skills must be generic and operate on any Odoo 17 module. Use addon roots, manifests, model names and inheritance chains discovered at runtime via the codebase MCP — never embed concrete module names into invariant rules.

decisions.question: How do we keep recurring answers consistent across sessions?
decisions.answer: Recurring 'how do we do X' answers come from this canonical decisions registry, not re-derived per conversation. Before answering such a question, call codebase.lookup_canonical_decision; if no match, propose adding an entry rather than guessing.
